metadata.description: Use Case 1: Demonstrates deterministic point-in-time retrieval. The constitutional text of Art. 6 (social rights) was amended three times (EC 26/2000, EC 64/2010, EC 90/2015), each producing a new Statutory Version that supersedes the previous one. No judicial interpretation overlays exist. A query for 2001-05-20 returns exactly one version: the one produced by EC 26/2000.
Enumerates the social rights guaranteed by the Brazilian Federal Constitution
Added 'moradia' (housing) to the list of social rights
Added 'alimentação' (food) to the list of social rights
Added 'transporte' (transportation) to the list of social rights

textUnits.content: São direitos sociais a educação, a saúde, o trabalho, o lazer, a segurança, a previdência social, a proteção à maternidade e à infância, a assistência aos desamparados, na forma desta Constituição.
The social rights are education, health, work, leisure, security, social security, protection of motherhood and childhood, and assistance to the destitute, as set forth in this Constitution.
Original 1988 text. Lists 9 social rights. Does not include housing (moradia), food (alimentação), or transportation (transporte).

São direitos sociais a educação, a saúde, o trabalho, a moradia, o lazer, a segurança, a previdência social, a proteção à maternidade e à infância, a assistência aos desamparados, na forma desta Constituição.
The social rights are education, health, work, housing, leisure, security, social security, protection of motherhood and childhood, and assistance to the destitute, as set forth in this Constitution.
EC 26/2000 added 'moradia' (housing). This version was valid from 2000-02-14 to 2010-02-03. A query for 2001-05-20 returns this version — the example from Use Case 1.

São direitos sociais a educação, a saúde, a alimentação, o trabalho, a moradia, o lazer, a segurança, a previdência social, a proteção à maternidade e à infância, a assistência aos desamparados, na forma desta Constituição.

The social rights are education, health, food, work, housing, leisure, security, social security, protection of motherhood and childhood, and assistance to the destitute, as set forth in this Constitution.
EC 64/2010 added 'alimentação' (food). This version was valid from 2010-02-04 to 2015-09-14.

São direitos sociais a educação, a saúde, a alimentação, o trabalho, a moradia, o transporte, o lazer, a segurança, a previdência social, a proteção à maternidade e à infância, a assistência aos desamparados, na forma desta Constituição.

The social rights are education, health, food, work, housing, transportation, leisure, security, social security, protection of motherhood and childhood, and assistance to the destitute, as set forth in this Constitution.
EC 90/2015 added 'transporte' (transportation). Current text as of 2015-09-15. No further amendments.

metadata.label: EC 26/2000 — adds 'moradia'
metadata.purpose: Adds housing (moradia) as an explicit social right

metadata.label: EC 64/2010 — adds 'alimentação'
metadata.purpose: Adds food (alimentação) as an explicit social right

metadata.label: EC 90/2015 — adds 'transporte'
metadata.purpose: Adds transportation (transporte) as an explicit social right

sampleQueries.interpretation: On May 20, 2001, only one version is valid: the one produced by EC 26/2000. The original (1988) version was terminated on 2000-02-13. No judicial interpretation exists, so |V_valid| = 1. This is the use case from the research paper: deterministic point-in-time retrieval.
Before EC 26/2000, the original 1988 text applies. Housing (moradia) is not a listed social right.
After EC 90/2015, the current text applies. All four additions (moradia, alimentação, transporte) are present. No judicial interpretation exists, so |V_valid| = 1.

Three Amendment actions in chronological order. Each terminates the previous S-TV and creates a new one. Unlike judicial interpretations (which overlay), legislative amendments supersede: at any point in time, exactly one S-TV is valid (|V_valid| = 1).

notes.contrastWithCaseLaw: Unlike the case law examples (HC 126.292, ADI 4983, IPI), this case has no judicial Interpretive Versions. Every action is a legislative Amendment, and each amendment terminates the prior S-TV before creating the new one. The result is always |V_valid| = 1 — a single, unambiguous text at any query date. This is the baseline scenario that motivated the SAT-Graph model: even without judicial overlays, temporal retrieval must be version-aware.
notes.pointInTime: The research paper's Use Case 1 queries this item at 2001-05-20, which falls within the EC 26/2000 window (2000-02-14 to 2010-02-03). The deterministic answer: 'moradia' is a social right; 'alimentação' and 'transporte' are not yet.
notes.topologyInsight: Legislative amendments follow the superseding model: S-TV_n → Amendment → S-TV_{n+1}. The old S-TV is terminated (terminatesVersionIds is non-empty). This is the opposite of judicial interpretations, which follow the overlay model (terminatesVersionIds: []).
